description: retry WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.25 on the depth-2 checkpoint (was catastrophic for depth-3 but depth-2 has ~50% more steps)
hypothesis: depth-2 gets ~170 steps; 0.25 warmdown means ~43 steps of cooldown which may be adequate unlike for depth-3
next_move: if still terrible, 0.30 is the floor regardless of depth; if better, try 0.20

replacements.old: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.30   # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown
replacements.new: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.25   # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown

description: try WARMDOWN_RATIO=0.35 on depth-2 — slightly more cooldown
hypothesis: depth-2 with more steps may benefit from a slightly longer cooldown that gives smoother final convergence
next_move: if worse, 0.30 is optimal; if better, try 0.40

replacements.old: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.30   # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown
replacements.new: WARMDOWN_RATIO = 0.35   # fraction of time budget for LR warmdown

description: retry MATRIX_LR=0.025 on depth-2 (was worse for depth-3 but depth-2 has different gradient dynamics)
hypothesis: with 2 layers the Muon updates are applied more frequently per token; a gentler step may work better
next_move: if worse, 0.03 is correct for depth-2 as well

replacements.old: MATRIX_LR = 0.03        # learning rate for matrix parameters (Muon)
replacements.new: MATRIX_LR = 0.025       # learning rate for matrix parameters (Muon)

description: try MATRIX_LR=0.035 on depth-2 — slightly more aggressive Muon
hypothesis: depth-2 with more gradient steps may tolerate a slightly higher Muon LR; we only tried 0.03 and 0.032 before on depth-3
next_move: if better, try 0.04; if worse, 0.03 is correct

replacements.old: MATRIX_LR = 0.03        # learning rate for matrix parameters (Muon)
replacements.new: MATRIX_LR = 0.035       # learning rate for matrix parameters (Muon)

description: retry SCALAR_LR=0.20 on depth-2 (was much worse for depth-3 but depth-2 has different convergence speed)
hypothesis: with ~170 steps, per-layer scalars may converge fine with a gentler 0.20 update rate
next_move: if worse, 0.25 is correct; if better, try 0.15

replacements.old: SCALAR_LR = 0.25        # learning rate for per-layer scalars (Adam)
replacements.new: SCALAR_LR = 0.20        # learning rate for per-layer scalars (Adam)

commit_message: DEPTH=1 ASPECT_RATIO=192 — single layer maximum steps
description: set DEPTH=1 and ASPECT_RATIO=192 (model_dim=256, 1 layer, potentially 3x more steps than depth-3)
hypothesis: if the depth-3→depth-2 improvement comes purely from more gradient steps, depth-1 should win; tests the capacity floor
next_move: if competitive, tune LRs for depth-1; if badly worse, depth-2 is the sweet spot for capacity vs. steps
